heading: Technical assistance and data collection support
label: 3005 Technical assistance and data collection support

children.heading: Qualified data collection and reporting entity
children.label: 1 Qualified data collection and reporting entity
A
children.text: provide recipients of grants under this chapter with instruction and technical assistance; and

children.label: B
children.text: assist such recipients with data collection and data requirements.

children.heading: Qualified protection and advocacy system technical assistance provider
children.label: 2 Qualified protection and advocacy system technical assistance provider
A
children.text: working with protection and advocacy systems established in accordance with ; andsection 15043 of title 42

children.label: B
children.text: providing technical assistance to protection and advocacy agencies.

children.label: A
children.text: experience and expertise in administering programs, including developing, implementing, and administering all of the activities described in ; andsection 3003(e) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: assistive technology device loan and demonstration;

children.label: ii
children.text: assistive technology device reuse;

children.label: iii
children.text: financial loans and microlending, including the activities of alternative financing programs for assistive technology; and

children.label: iv
children.text: State leadership activities.

children.heading: Technical assistance and data collection support authorized
children.label: b Technical assistance and data collection support authorized

children.heading: Support for assistive technology educational activities and technical assistance
children.label: 1 Support for assistive technology educational activities and technical assistance
A
children.text: to qualified technical assistance providers to support activities described in subsection (d)(1) for States receiving grants under ; andsection 3003 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: to qualified protection and advocacy system technical assistance providers to support activities described in subsection (d)(1) for protection and advocacy systems receiving grants under .section 3004 of this title

children.heading: Support for data collection and reporting assistance
children.label: 2 Support for data collection and reporting assistance
A
children.text: to qualified data collection and reporting entities, to enable the qualified data collection and reporting entities to carry out the activities described in subsection (d)(2) for States receiving grants under ; andsection 3003 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: to qualified protection and advocacy system technical assistance providers, to enable the providers to carry out the activities described in subsection (d)(2) for protection and advocacy systems receiving grants under .section 3004 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: A description of the activities such entity will carry out with the grant, contract, or cooperative agreement under subsection (d).

children.label: B
children.text: A description of the expertise such entity has to carry out such activities.

children.label: C
children.text: In the case of an entity applying to receive a grant, contract, or cooperative agreement under subsection (b)(1), a description of such entity’s plan for complying with the requirements described in subsection (d)(1)(B).

children.label: D
children.text: A description of such entity’s plan to comply with all relevant State and Federal laws, regulations, and policies with respect to data privacy and security.

children.label: E
children.text: Such other information as the Secretary may require.

children.label: A
children.text: individuals with disabilities who use assistive technology and understand the barriers to the acquisition of such technology and assistive technology services;

children.label: B
children.text: family members, guardians, advocates, and authorized representatives of such individuals;

children.label: C
children.text: relevant employees from Federal departments and agencies, other than the Department of Health and Human Services;

children.label: D
children.text: representatives of businesses; and

children.label: E
children.text: venders and public and private researchers and developers.

children.heading: Use of funds for assistive technology technical assistance
children.label: 1 Use of funds for assistive technology technical assistance
I
children.text: effective approaches to Federal-State coordination of programs for individuals with disabilities related to improving funding for or access to assistive technology devices and assistive technology services for individuals with disabilities;

children.label: II
children.text: model State and local laws, regulations, policies, practices, procedures, and organizational structures, that facilitate, and overcome barriers to, funding for, and access to, assistive technology devices and assistive technology services;

children.label: III
children.text: effective approaches to developing, implementing, evaluating, and sustaining activities described in section 3003 or 3004 of this title, as the case may be, and related to improving acquisition and access to assistive technology devices and assistive technology services for individuals with disabilities, and requests for assistance in developing corrective action plans;

children.label: IV
children.text: policies, practices, procedures, regulations, or judicial decisions related to access to and acquisition of assistive technology devices and assistive technology services for individuals with disabilities;

children.label: V
children.text: effective approaches to the development of consumer-controlled systems that increase access to, funding for, and awareness of, assistive technology devices and assistive technology services; and

children.label: VI
children.text: other requests for information and technical assistance from entities funded under this chapter; and

children.label: I
children.text: assists targeted individuals and entities by disseminating information and responding to requests relating to assistive technology by providing referrals to recipients of grants under or other public or private resources; andsection 3003 of this title

children.label: aa
children.text: annually providing a forum for exchanging information concerning, and promoting program and policy improvements in, required activities of the State assistive technology programs;

children.label: bb
children.text: facilitating onsite and electronic information sharing using state-of-the-art internet technologies such as real-time online discussions, multipoint video conferencing, and web-based audio or video broadcasts, on emerging topics that affect State assistive technology programs;

children.label: cc
children.text: convening experts from State assistive technology programs to discuss and make recommendations with regard to national emerging issues of importance to individuals with assistive technology needs;

children.label: dd
children.text: sharing best practice and evidence-based practices among State assistive technology programs;

children.label: ee
children.text: developing or maintaining an accessible, national, and public website that includes information, tools, and resources on assistive technology devices and assistive technology services and links to State assistive technology programs, appropriate Federal departments and agencies, and private resources;

children.label: ff
children.text: developing a resource that connects individuals from a State with the State assistive technology program in their State;

children.label: gg
children.text: providing access to experts in the State-level activities described in through site visits, teleconferences, and other means, to ensure access to information for entities that are carrying out new programs or programs that are not making progress in achieving the objectives of the programs; andsection 3003(e)(2) of this title

children.label: hh
children.text: supporting and coordinating activities designed to reduce the financial costs of purchasing assistive technology for the activities described in , and reducing duplication of activities among State assistive technology programs.section 3003(e) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: organizations representing individuals with disabilities;

children.label: II
children.text: national organizations representing State assistive technology programs;

children.label: III
children.text: organizations representing State officials and agencies engaged in the delivery of assistive technology;

children.label: IV
children.text: other qualified protection and advocacy system technical assistance providers and qualified technical assistance providers;

children.label: V
children.text: providers of State financing activities, including alternative financing programs for assistive technology;

children.label: VI
children.text: providers of device loans, device demonstrations, and device reutilization; and

children.label: VII
children.text: any other organizations determined appropriate by the provider or the Secretary; and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a qualified technical assistance provider, include activities identified as priorities by State advisory councils and lead agencies and implementing entities for grants under .section 3003 of this title

children.heading: Use of funds for assistive technology data collection and reporting assistance
children.label: 2 Use of funds for assistive technology data collection and reporting assistance
A
children.text: focus on quantitative and qualitative data elements;

children.label: B
children.text: help measure the impact of the activities to individuals who need assistive technology;

children.label: i
children.text: measure the outcomes of all activities described in and the progress of the States toward achieving the measurable goals described in ; andsection 3003(e) of this titlesection 3003(d)(3)(C) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: provide States with the necessary information required under this chapter or by the Secretary for reports described in ; andsection 3003(f)(2) of this title

children.label: D
children.text: are in full compliance with all relevant State and Federal laws, regulations, and policies with respect to data privacy and security.
